Google Cloud: Google Cloud is quite popular among startups as it offers various discounts for cloud users. Google Cloud is better for you if pricing is a significant aspect. Azure offers immense flexibility, and AWS has the edge over the others with its massive global footprint.

In my experience GCP is for instance definitely cheaper with larger CloudSQL instances vs AWS RDS. When hosting at smaller scales it can be tricky to keep costs down as things GKE can have a fairly high base cost if you choose the wrong option or put a foot outside the free tier boundries.

Hi there, Yes, getting a Scrum Master certification can definitely help you land your first Scrum job. It shows employers that you have a formal understanding of Scrum principles. Given your experience in SAAS sales and management, you already have valuable skills to bring to the role.

There's no benefit to a PSM-III over a PSM-I when it comes to hiring, at least in my experience. Honestly, there's probably not much benefit at all to a PSM-III over a PSM-I, except in terms of money for the certifying body. Anything they teach you in class you can learn for yourself.

Best bang for your buck in order: PmP (you may not have the requisite experience just yet, but def worth it) CSM SAFE CAPM And I’ll throw a wildcard Security + because you work in defense consulting. You will need an IAT Level II cert to move up.
